fields.description:
# Authenticated identity — entity + authorship certification + provider-neutral user mapping

## Context
rebar has no real concept of **who** performed an operation: `author` is `git config user.name`, an
unauthenticated free string anyone with push access to `origin/tickets` can forge. Two needs follow:
(1) attribute ticket events to a **real person** so activity is auditable and non-repudiable, and
(2) map that person to **external ticket-system users** (Jira now; GitHub Issues / Linear later) so
sync assigns work to the right person. **Beneficiary:** projects syncing to external trackers (correct
assignee/reporter attribution today, wrong or lost today) and anyone auditing "who did this." This
epic depends on the **asymmetric attestation foundation** for its signing substrate.

## Approach (and why)
Adopt **git-bug's** identity model on rebar's existing event machinery, filling its gaps:
- **Identity = a first-class entity** whose event log IS its version chain (rebar already has
  event-sourcing / replay / snapshot). Stable **opaque rebar id** as the handle — **not**
  self-certifying; the signing key provides tamper-evidence, not the id (simpler than git-bug and it
  fits rebar's existing id minting). Attributes: `name`, `email`, `{provider, external_id}` external
  mappings, and public keys.
- **Attribution always** (name+email denormalized on every event, git-style) + an optional
  `author_id` reference + an optional **authorship signature** — a new attestation kind on the
  foundation's substrate (namespace `rebar.authorship.v1`).
- **Trust root = the identity's in-band keys**, TOFU on the genesis key + **G2** (key add/rotate/
  revoke must be signed by a currently-valid key). Safe for authorship specifically: low authority,
  cannot forge another person's signatures; the worst case from a bad TOFU binding is a display-name
  dispute, not a forged event. Key validity is scoped to **merged-log position (era)**, not
  attacker-controllable wall-clock event timestamps.
- **Provider-neutral mapping.** `{provider, external_id}` keys on the provider's **opaque stable id**,
  never email (Jira `accountId` is null/hidden under privacy settings; OIDC/SCIM forbid email as the
  key). Jira is the **first adapter** behind a provider seam; an unmapped inbound user mints a
  **placeholder** that stores the raw `(provider, external_id, display_name)` and **idempotently
  auto-upgrades** when a real mapping appears (Gitea `OriginalAuthorID` discipline).
- **Deliberate creation** (explicit registration, or minted as a mapping target) — no auto-published
  per-write registry, so a contributor's email is never broadcast to the shared branch as a side
  effect of a normal write.
- **Enforcement opt-in per project and enforced at the MERGE gate** (the local write-gate is a
  fast-fail UX affordance, **not** the control — any clone can disable it); replay **never** rejects
  (mark verified / unverified / unsigned; verification runs in fsck + the merge gate).

## Scope
- **In:** the `identity` entity (a new ticket-type on the `session_log` exemption pattern);
  event attribution fields; the authorship attestation kind on the foundation's substrate; the
  TOFU + G2 key chain with era-scoped validity; the provider-neutral mapping + Jira adapter +
  placeholder/auto-upgrade; reconciler outbound assignee/reporter + inbound ghost; opt-in enforcement
  (write-gate + merge-gate verify + fsck marking); snapshot/compaction carrying folded identities +
  signatures; docs + ADR.
- **Out:** the signing substrate itself (foundation epic); operation-cert behavior (op-cert epic);
  DSSE transparency-log / keyless follow-ons; **actual** GitHub Issues / Linear sync (the adapter
  seam must not preclude them, but wiring them is separate).

## Success Criteria
- A person running rebar has a modeled identity; events attribute it (name+email always; an
  authorship signature when opted in), verified offline against the identity's keys.
- An opt-in project can REQUIRE authenticated authorship: unsigned / unknown-author events are
  flagged by fsck and rejected at the merge gate, while replay never rejects (availability preserved).
- The identity provides a stable local↔`accountId` map the reconciler uses to set the Jira assignee
  (and reporter where permitted), degrading gracefully when `accountId` can't be resolved; unmapped
  inbound Jira users become auto-upgrading placeholders.
- Key rotation works: events verify against the keys valid at their log-era; a revoked key cannot
  sign new events.
- Default install unchanged; signing and enforcement are fully opt-in.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A new `identity` ticket-type exists and is exempt from lifecycle / gates / graph / Jira-sync
      (mirrors `session_log`); a create path (`rebar identity <name> --email <e>` or equivalent) mints
      one carrying name, email, `{provider,external_id}` mappings, and public keys. Verified by
      creating one, asserting `show` renders the fields, and asserting it is excluded from
      `ready` / `next_batch` / default `list`.
- [ ] Every mutating event carries a denormalized `author` name + email; an optional `author_id`
      references an identity. Regression test: existing events without `author_id` still replay.
- [ ] An `authorship` kind (namespace `rebar.authorship.v1`) signs the event's DSSE-PAE bytes via the
      foundation's substrate and verifies offline against the identity's in-band keys; a test asserts
      an event signed by identity A does not verify against identity B's keys, and a tampered event
      fails.
- [ ] Key add/rotate/revoke versions are refused unless signed by a currently-valid key of that
      identity (G2); a test asserts an unsigned key-add is refused, and an event signed by a key
      revoked at an earlier era fails while one from the key's valid era passes.
- [ ] `{provider, external_id}` mappings key on the provider's opaque id; the reconciler resolves an
      outbound Jira assignee by `accountId`, bootstraps via `/user/search` best-effort, and degrades
      to project-default (`-1`) / unassigned without hard-failing when email is hidden. Test with a
      fixture identity + stubbed `accountId`.
- [ ] An inbound Jira user with no mapping creates a placeholder identity storing
      `(provider, external_id, display_name)`; re-running resolution after a real mapping is added
      idempotently upgrades the placeholder — a test asserts one identity results, not two.
- [ ] Enforcement is opt-in (`identity.require_authenticated` or similar): with it on, the merge-gate
      verification flags an unsigned / unknown-author event; the local write-gate fails fast but is
      documented as UX-only, not the control. Replay never rejects (test: a bad-signature event still
      folds into state) and `show`/`fsck` surface verified/unverified/unsigned.
- [ ] Snapshots (compaction) carry folded-event identities + signatures so compacted signed events
      still verify (test round-trips a compacted signed event).
- [ ] Docs + ADR: identity model, the tiers, opt-in enforcement + the merge-gate-is-the-control
      caveat, Jira `accountId` mapping + the Modify-Reporter constraint, key-rotation / era-scoping,
      and the provider-neutral seam.

## Intended decomposition (children)
1. `identity` ticket-type + entity (attributes, exemptions, creation, config pointer).
2. Event attribution (name+email + `author_id`) + replay back-compat.
3. Authorship attestation kind on the foundation's substrate (sign/verify vs in-band keys).
4. TOFU genesis + G2 signed key-rotation chain + era-scoped validity.
5. Provider-neutral mapping + Jira adapter (outbound assignee/reporter, bootstrap, degrade).
6. Inbound ghost/placeholder + idempotent auto-upgrade.
7. Opt-in enforcement (write-gate + merge-gate verify + fsck marking) + replay-never-rejects + snapshot carries identities/sigs.
8. Docs + ADR.

## Prior art / grounding
git-bug identity entity + bridge metadata; SCIM `externalId`; OIDC `(iss,sub)` (email disqualified as
key); Jira Cloud GDPR `accountId` (email null/hidden); Gitea/GitLab placeholder-user + auto-upgrade;
git `.mailmap` (email as adopt-heuristic only). Depends on the asymmetric attestation foundation.
fields.title: Authenticated identity — entity + authorship certification + provider-neutral user mapping
